content.thinking: Let me start by gathering the cluster information needed for the audit. I'll run all the inspection commands in parallel to collect the data efficiently.

content.text: I'll gather all the cluster information in parallel, then write the findings to the ConfigMap.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure 2>&1
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT variable, value FROM crdb_internal.cluster_settings WHERE value != default_value;" 2>&1
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT target, raw_config_sql FROM crdb_internal.zones;" 2>&1
kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o wide 2>&1

content.thinking: Now I have all the information I need. Let me also check cluster health and any additional settings.

content.text: Good data collected. Let me also grab cluster health status and any sql.defaults.* settings specifically.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT variable, value, default_value, description FROM crdb_internal.cluster_settings WHERE variable LIKE 'sql.defaults.%' OR variable LIKE 'kv.%' OR variable LIKE 'server.%' ORDER BY variable;" 2>&1
input.description: Get sql.defaults and kv cluster settings with descriptions

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT health FROM crdb_internal.node_runtime_info LIMIT 1;" 2>&1 || kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT node_id, is_available, is_live FROM crdb_internal.gossip_nodes;" 2>&1

variable	value	default_value	description
kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold	0.3	0.3	a store will not receive new leases when its IO overload score is above this value and `kv.allocator.io_overload_threshold` is `shed` or `block_transfer_to`
kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold_enforcement	shed	shed	the level of enforcement on lease transfers when a candidate store has anio overload score exceeding `kv.allocator.io_overload_threshold_lease` and above the average of comparable allocation candidates:`ignore` disable enforcement, `block_transfer_to` a store will receive no new leases but won't lose existing leases,`shed`: a store will receive no new leases and shed existing leases to non io-overloaded stores, this is a superset of block_transfer_to [ignore = 0, block_transfer_to = 1, shed = 3]
kv.allocator.lease_rebalance_threshold	0.05	0.05	minimum fraction away from the mean a store's lease count can be before it is considered for lease-transfers
kv.allocator.lease_rebalancing_aggressiveness	1	1	set greater than 1.0 to rebalance leases toward load more aggressively, or between 0 and 1.0 to be more conservative about rebalancing leases
kv.allocator.lease_shed_io_overload_threshold	0.4	0.4	a store will shed its leases and receive no new leases when its IO overload score is above this value and `kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold_enforcement` is `shed`
kv.allocator.load_based_lease_rebalancing.enabled	true	true	set to enable rebalancing of range leases based on load and latency
kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing	leases and replicas	leases and replicas	whether to rebalance based on the distribution of load across stores [off = 0, leases = 1, leases and replicas = 2]
kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing.objective	cpu	cpu	what objective does the cluster use to rebalance; if set to `qps` the cluster will attempt to balance qps among stores, if set to `cpu` the cluster will attempt to balance cpu usage among stores [qps = 0, cpu = 1]
...
</persisted-output>

tool_use_result.stdout:
variable	value	default_value	description
kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold	0.3	0.3	a store will not receive new leases when its IO overload score is above this value and `kv.allocator.io_overload_threshold` is `shed` or `block_transfer_to`
kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold_enforcement	shed	shed	the level of enforcement on lease transfers when a candidate store has anio overload score exceeding `kv.allocator.io_overload_threshold_lease` and above the average of comparable allocation candidates:`ignore` disable enforcement, `block_transfer_to` a store will receive no new leases but won't lose existing leases,`shed`: a store will receive no new leases and shed existing leases to non io-overloaded stores, this is a superset of block_transfer_to [ignore = 0, block_transfer_to = 1, shed = 3]
kv.allocator.lease_rebalance_threshold	0.05	0.05	minimum fraction away from the mean a store's lease count can be before it is considered for lease-transfers
kv.allocator.lease_rebalancing_aggressiveness	1	1	set greater than 1.0 to rebalance leases toward load more aggressively, or between 0 and 1.0 to be more conservative about rebalancing leases
kv.allocator.lease_shed_io_overload_threshold	0.4	0.4	a store will shed its leases and receive no new leases when its IO overload score is above this value and `kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold_enforcement` is `shed`
kv.allocator.load_based_lease_rebalancing.enabled	true	true	set to enable rebalancing of range leases based on load and latency
kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing	leases and replicas	leases and replicas	whether to rebalance based on the distribution of load across stores [off = 0, leases = 1, leases and replicas = 2]
kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing.objective	cpu	cpu	what objective does the cluster use to rebalance; if set to `qps` the cluster will attempt to balance qps among stores, if set to `cpu` the cluster will attempt to balance cpu usage among stores [qps = 0, cpu = 1]
kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing_interval	1m0s	1m0s	the rough interval at which each store will check for load-based lease / replica rebalancing opportunities
kv.allocator.max_disk_utilization_threshold	0.95	0.95	maximum disk utilization before a store will never be used as a rebalance or allocation target and will actively have replicas moved off of it; this should be set higher than `kv.allocator.rebalance_to_max_disk_utilization_threshold`
kv.allocator.min_io_overload_lease_shed_interval	30s	30s	controls how frequently all leases can be shed from a node due to the node becoming IO overloaded
kv.allocator.min_lease_transfer_interval	1s	1s	controls how frequently leases can be transferred for rebalancing. It does not prevent transferring leases in order to allow a replica to be removed from a range.
kv.allocator.min_qps_difference_for_transfers	200	200	the minimum qps difference that must exist between any two stores for the allocator to allow a lease or replica transfer between them
kv.allocator.qps_rebalance_threshold	0.1	0.1	minimum fraction away from the mean a store's QPS (such as queries per second) can be before it is considered overfull or underfull
kv.allocator.range_rebalance_threshold	0.05	0.05	minimum fraction away from the mean a store's range count can be before it is considered overfull or underfull
kv.allocator.rebalance_to_max_disk_utilization_threshold	0.925	0.925	maximum disk utilization before a store will never be used as a rebalance target; this should be set lower than `kv.allocator.max_disk_utilization_threshold`
kv.allocator.recovery_store_selector	good	good	if set to 'good', the allocator may recover replicas to any valid store, if set to 'best' it will pick one of the most ideal stores
kv.allocator.replica_io_overload_threshold	0.3	0.3	the maximum store io overload before the enforcement defined by `kv.allocator.io_overload_threshold_enforce` is taken on a store for allocation decisions
kv.allocator.replica_io_overload_threshold_enforcement	block_rebalance_to	block_rebalance_to	the level of enforcement when a candidate store has an io overload score  exceeding `kv.allocator.io_overload_threshold` and above the average of comparable allocation candidates:`block_none` will exclude no candidate stores, `block_none_log` will exclude no candidates but log an event, `block_rebalance_to` will exclude candidates stores from being targets of rebalance actions, `block_all` will exclude candidate stores from being targets of both allocation and rebalancing [ignore = 0, block_rebalance_to = 1, block_all = 2]
kv.allocator.store_cpu_rebalance_threshold	0.1	0.1	minimum fraction away from the mean a store's cpu usage can be before it is considered overfull or underfull
kv.bulk_ingest.batch_size	16 MiB	16 MiB	the maximum size of the payload in an AddSSTable request
kv.bulk_ingest.index_buffer_size	32 MiB	32 MiB	the initial size of the BulkAdder buffer handling secondary index imports
kv.bulk_ingest.max_index_buffer_size	512 MiB	512 MiB	the maximum size of the BulkAdder buffer handling secondary index imports
kv.bulk_ingest.max_pk_buffer_size	128 MiB	128 MiB	the maximum size of the BulkAdder buffer handling primary index imports
kv.bulk_ingest.pk_buffer_size	32 MiB	32 MiB	the initial size of the BulkAdder buffer handling primary index imports
kv.bulk_ingest.stream_external_ssts.enabled	true	true	if enabled, external SSTables are iterated directly in some cases, rather than being downloaded entirely first
kv.bulk_ingest.stream_external_ssts.suffix_cache_size	64 KiB	64 KiB	size of suffix of remote SSTs to download and cache before reading from remote stream
kv.bulk_io_write.concurrent_addsstable_as_writes_requests	9223372036854775807	9223372036854775807	number of concurrent AddSSTable requests ingested as writes per store before queueing
kv.bulk_io_write.concurrent_addsstable_requests	9223372036854775807	9223372036854775807	number of concurrent AddSSTable requests per store before queueing
kv.bulk_io_write.concurrent_export_requests	3	3	number of export requests a store will handle concurrently before queuing
kv.bulk_io_write.max_rate	1.0 TiB	1.0 TiB	the rate limit (bytes/sec) to use for writes to disk on behalf of bulk io ops
kv.bulk_io_write.min_capacity_remaining_fraction	0.05	0.05	remaining store capacity fraction below which an addsstable request is rejected
kv.bulk_io_write.restore_node_concurrency	4	4	the number of workers processing a restore per job per node; maximum 32
kv.bulk_io_write.small_write_size	400 KiB	400 KiB	size below which a 'bulk' write will be performed as a normal write instead
kv.bulk_io_write.sst_require_at_request_timestamp.enabled	false	false	rejects addsstable requests that don't write at the request timestamp
kv.bulk_io_write.sst_rewrite_concurrency.per_call	4	4	concurrency to use when rewriting sstable timestamps by block, or 0 to use a loop
kv.bulk_sst.max_allowed_overage	64 MiB	64 MiB	if positive, allowed size in excess of target size for SSTs from export requests; export requests (i.e. BACKUP) may buffer up to the sum of kv.bulk_sst.target_size and kv.bulk_sst.max_allowed_overage in memory
kv.bulk_sst.sync_size	512 KiB	512 KiB	threshold after which non-Rocks SST writes must fsync (0 disables)
kv.bulk_sst.target_size	16 MiB	16 MiB	target size for SSTs emitted from export requests; export requests (i.e. BACKUP) may buffer up to the sum of kv.bulk_sst.target_size and kv.bulk_sst.max_allowed_overage in memory
kv.bulkio.write_metadata_sst.enabled	false	false	write experimental new format BACKUP metadata file
kv.closed_timestamp.follower_reads.enabled	true	true	allow (all) replicas to serve consistent historical reads based on closed timestamp information
kv.closed_timestamp.lead_for_global_reads_override	0s	0s	if nonzero, overrides the lead time that global_read ranges use to publish closed timestamps
kv.closed_timestamp.propagation_slack	1s	1s	a conservative estimate of the amount of time expect for closed timestamps to propagate from a leaseholder to followers. This is taken into account by follower_read_timestamp().
kv.closed_timestamp.side_transport_interval	200ms	200ms	the interval at which the closed timestamp side-transport attempts to advance each range's closed timestamp; set to 0 to disable the side-transport
kv.closed_timestamp.target_duration	3s	3s	if nonzero, attempt to provide closed timestamp notifications for timestamps trailing cluster time by approximately this duration
kv.concurrency.long_latch_hold_duration	3s	3s	the threshold for logging long latch holds
kv.concurrency.optimistic_eval_limited_scans.enabled	true	true	when true, limited scans are optimistically evaluated in the sense of not checking for conflicting latches or locks up front for the full key range of the scan, and instead subsequently checking for conflicts only over the key range that was read
kv.consistency_queue.enabled	true	true	whether the consistency queue is enabled
kv.dist_sender.circuit_breaker.cancellation.enabled	true	true	when enabled, in-flight requests will be cancelled when the circuit breaker trips
kv.dist_sender.circuit_breaker.cancellation.write_grace_period	10s	10s	how long after the circuit breaker trips to cancel write requests (these can't retry internally, so should be long enough to allow quorum/lease recovery)
kv.dist_sender.circuit_breaker.probe.interval	3s	3s	interval between replica probes
kv.dist_sender.circuit_breaker.probe.threshold	3s	3s	duration of errors or stalls after which a replica will be probed
kv.dist_sender.circuit_breaker.probe.timeout	3s	3s	timeout for replica probes
kv.dist_sender.circuit_breakers.mode	no ranges	no ranges	set of ranges to trip circuit breakers for failing or stalled replicas [no ranges = 0, liveness range only = 1, all ranges = 2]
kv.dist_sender.concurrency_limit	2560	2560	maximum number of asynchronous send requests
kv.dist_sender.follower_reads_unhealthy.enabled	true	true	send follower reads to unhealthy nodes
kv.dist_sender.proxy.enabled	true	true	when true, proxy batch requests that can't be routed directly to the leaseholder
kv.dist_sender.sort_locality_first.enabled	true	true	sort followers by locality before sorting by latency
kv.enqueue_in_mvcc_gc_queue_on_span_config_update.enabled	false	false	controls whether replicas are enqueued into the mvcc gc queue for processing, when a span config update occurs which affects the replica
kv.enqueue_in_replicate_queue_on_span_config_update.enabled	true	true	controls whether replicas are enqueued into the replicate queue for processing, when a span config update occurs, which affects the replica
kv.expiration_leases_only.enabled	false	false	only use expiration-based leases never epoch-based ones when there are less than kv.lease.expiration_max_replicas_per_node on the node, (experimental, affects performance)
kv.gc.admission_priority	bulk_normal_pri	bulk_normal_pri	the admission priority to use for mvcc gc work [bulk_normal_pri = -30, normal_pri = 0, user_high_pri = 50]
kv.gc.clear_range_min_keys	2000	2000	if non zero, gc will issue clear range requests if number of consecutive garbage keys exceeds this threshold
kv.gc.lock_age_threshold	2h0m0s	2h0m0s	locks older than this threshold will be resolved when encountered by the MVCC GC queue
kv.gc.lock_cleanup_batch_byte_size	1000000	1000000	if non zero, gc will split found locks into batches of this size when trying to resolve them
kv.gc.lock_cleanup_batch_size	5000	5000	if non zero, gc will split found locks into batches of this size when trying to resolve them
kv.gc.sticky_hint.enabled	false	false	enable writing sticky GC hints which expedite garbage collection after schema changes (ignored and assumed 'true' in 23.2)
kv.gc.txn_cleanup_threshold	1h0m0s	1h0m0s	the threshold after which a transaction is considered abandoned and fit for removal, as measured by the maximum of its last heartbeat and timestamp
kv.gc_ttl.strict_enforcement.enabled	true	true	if true, fail to serve requests at timestamps below the TTL even if the data still exists
kv.intent_resolver.batch.bypass_admission_control.enabled	false	false	determined whether batched intent resolution bypasses admission control
kv.intent_resolver.batcher.in_flight_backpressure_limit.enabled	false	false	set to true to enable the use of DefaultInFlightBackpressureLimit
kv.intent_resolver.send_immediately.bypass_admission_control.enabled	true	true	determines whether the sendImmediately option on intent resolution bypasses admission control
kv.lease.check_preferences_on_acquisition.enabled	true	true	controls whether lease preferences are checked on lease acquisition, if the new lease violates preferences, it is queued for processing
kv.lease.eager_acquisition_concurrency	256	256	the maximum number of concurrent eager lease acquisitions (0 disables eager acquisition)
kv.lease.expiration_max_replicas_per_node	0	0	maximum number of replicas a node can have before expiration leases are disabled (0 disables this setting)
kv.lease.reject_on_leader_unknown.enabled	false	false	reject lease requests on a replica that does not know the raft leader
kv.lease_queue.enabled	true	true	whether the lease queue is enabled
kv.lease_transfer_read_summary.global_budget	0 B	0 B	controls the maximum number of bytes that will be used to summarize the global segment of the timestamp cache during lease transfers and range merges. A smaller budget will result in loss of precision.
kv.lease_transfer_read_summary.local_budget	4.0 MiB	4.0 MiB	controls the maximum number of bytes that will be used to summarize the local segment of the timestamp cache during lease transfers and range merges. A smaller budget will result in loss of precision.
kv.lock.exclusive_locks_block_non_locking_reads.enabled	true	true	dictates the locking interactions between exclusive locks and non-locking reads
kv.lock_table.batch_pushed_lock_resolution.enabled	true	true	whether the lock table should allow non-locking readers to defer and batch the resolution of conflicting locks whose holder is known to be pending and have been pushed above the reader's timestamp
kv.lock_table.deadlock_detection_or_liveness_push_delay	100ms	100ms	the delay before pushing in order to detect dependency cycles between transactions
kv.lock_table.discovered_locks_threshold_for_consulting_finalized_txn_cache	200	200	the maximum number of discovered locks by a waiter, above which the txn status cacheis consulted and resolvable locks are not added to the lock table -- this should be a smallfraction of the maximum number of locks in the lock table
kv.lock_table.maximum_lock_wait_queue_length	0	0	the maximum length of a lock wait-queue that read-write requests are willing to enter and wait in. The setting can be used to ensure some level of quality-of-service under severe per-key contention. If set to a non-zero value and an existing lock wait-queue is already equal to or exceeding this length, requests will be rejected eagerly instead of entering the queue and waiting. Set to 0 to disable.
kv.log_range_and_node_events.enabled	true	true	set to true to transactionally log range events (e.g., split, merge, add/remove voter/non-voter) into system.rangelogand node join and restart events into system.eventolog
kv.migration.migrate_application.timeout	1m0s	1m0s	timeout for a Migrate request to be applied across all replicas of a range
kv.mvcc_gc.queue_high_priority_interval	0s	0s	how long the mvcc gc queue waits between processing high priority replicas (e.g. after table drops)
kv.mvcc_gc.queue_interval	1s	1s	how long the mvcc gc queue waits between processing replicas
kv.mvcc_gc_queue.enabled	true	true	whether the MVCC GC queue is enabled
kv.prober.bypass_admission_control.enabled	true	true	set to bypass admission control queue for kvprober requests; note that dedicated clusters should have this set as users own capacity planning but serverless clusters should not have this set as SREs own capacity planning
kv.prober.planner.num_steps_to_plan_at_once	100	100	the number of Steps to plan at once, where a Step is a decision on what range to probe; the order of the Steps is randomized within each planning run, so setting this to a small number will lead to close-to-lexical probing; already made plans are held in memory, so large values are advised against
kv.prober.planner.scan_meta2.timeout	2s	2s	timeout on scanning meta2 via db.Scan with max rows set to kv.prober.planner.num_steps_to_plan_at_once
kv.prober.quarantine.write.enabled	false	false	whether the KV write prober is enabled for the quarantine pool; The quarantine pool holds a separate group of ranges that have previously failed a probe which are continually probed. This helps determine outages for ranges  with a high level of confidence
kv.prober.quarantine.write.interval	10s	10s	how often each node sends a write probe for the quarantine pool to the KV layer on average (jitter is added); note that a very slow read can block kvprober from sending additional probes; kv.prober.write.timeout controls the max time kvprober can be blocked
kv.prober.quarantine_pool_size	100	100	the maximum size of the kv prober quarantine pool, where the quarantine pool holds Steps for ranges that have been probed and timed out; If the quarantine pool is full, probes that fail will not be added to  the pool
kv.prober.read.enabled	false	false	whether the KV read prober is enabled
kv.prober.read.interval	1s	1s	how often each node sends a read probe to the KV layer on average (jitter is added); note that a very slow read can block kvprober from sending additional probes; kv.prober.read.timeout controls the max time kvprober can be blocked
kv.prober.read.timeout	2s	2s	if this much time elapses without success, a KV read probe will be treated as an error; note that a very slow read can block kvprober from sending additional probesthis setting controls the max time kvprober can be blocked
kv.prober.write.enabled	false	false	whether the KV write prober is enabled
kv.prober.write.interval	5s	5s	how often each node sends a write probe to the KV layer on average (jitter is added); note that a very slow read can block kvprober from sending additional probes; kv.prober.write.timeout controls the max time kvprober can be blocked
kv.prober.write.timeout	4s	4s	if this much time elapses without success, a KV write probe will be treated as an error; note that a very slow read can block kvprober from sending additional probesthis setting controls the max time kvprober can be blocked
kv.protectedts.max_bytes	1048576	1048576	if non-zero, this limits the number of bytes used by protected timestamp records in the protected timestamps system table. this will be a noop in 24.1 onwards and deprecated in the future
kv.protectedts.max_spans	32768	32768	if non-zero the limit of the number of spans which can be protected
kv.protectedts.poll_interval	2m0s	2m0s	the interval at which the protectedts subsystem state is polled
kv.protectedts.reconciliation.interval	5m0s	5m0s	the frequency for reconciling jobs with protected timestamp records
kv.query_resolved_timestamp.intent_cleanup_age	10s	10s	minimum intent age that QueryResolvedTimestamp requests will consider for async intent cleanup
kv.queue.process.guaranteed_time_budget	1m0s	1m0s	the guaranteed duration before which the processing of a queue may time out
kv.raft.command.max_size	64 MiB	64 MiB	maximum size of a raft command
kv.raft.command.target_batch_size	64 MiB	64 MiB	size of a batch of raft commands after which it will be sent without further batching
kv.raft_log.loosely_coupled_truncation.enabled	false	false	set to true to loosely couple the raft log truncation
kv.raft_log.non_blocking_synchronization.enabled	true	true	set to true to enable non-blocking synchronization on Raft log writes to persistent storage. Setting to true does not risk data loss or data corruption on server crashes, but can reduce write latency.
kv.raft_log.synchronization.unsafe.disabled	false	false	disables synchronization of Raft log writes to persistent storage. Setting to true risks data loss or data corruption on process or OS crashes. This not only disables fsync, but also disables flushing writes to the OS buffer. The setting is meant for internal testing only and SHOULD NOT be used in production.
kv.raft_log_queue.enabled	true	true	whether the raft log queue is enabled
kv.raft_snapshot_queue.enabled	true	true	whether the raft snapshot queue is enabled
kv.range.backpressure_byte_tolerance	32 MiB	32 MiB	defines the number of bytes above the product of backpressure_range_size_multiplier and the range_max_size at which backpressure will not apply
kv.range.backpressure_range_size_multiplier	2	2	multiple of range_max_bytes that a range is allowed to grow to without splitting before writes to that range are blocked, or 0 to disable
kv.range_descriptor_cache.size	1000000	1000000	maximum number of entries in the range descriptor cache
kv.range_merge.queue.enabled	true	true	whether the automatic merge queue is enabled
kv.range_merge.queue_interval	5s	5s	how long the merge queue waits between processing replicas
kv.range_merge.skip_external_bytes.enabled	false	false	skip the merge queue for external bytes
kv.range_split.by_load.enabled	true	true	allow automatic splits of ranges based on where load is concentrated
kv.range_split.by_load_merge_delay	5m0s	5m0s	the delay that range splits created due to load will wait before considering being merged away
kv.range_split.estimated_stats_for_external_bytes.enabled	true	true	allow splits to use estimated stats when the range being split has external bytes in its underlying store
kv.range_split.load_cpu_threshold	500ms	500ms	the CPU use per second over which, the range becomes a candidate for load based splitting
kv.range_split.load_qps_threshold	2500	2500	the QPS over which, the range becomes a candidate for load based splitting
kv.rangefeed.client.stream_startup_rate	100	100	controls the rate per second the client will initiate new rangefeed stream for a single range; 0 implies unlimited
kv.rangefeed.closed_timestamp_refresh_interval	3s	3s	the interval at which closed-timestamp updatesare delivered to rangefeeds; set to 0 to use kv.closed_timestamp.side_transport_interval
kv.rangefeed.closed_timestamp_smear_interval	1ms	1ms	the target interval at which the rangefeed updater job wakes up to deliverclosed-timestamp updates to some rangefeeds; set to 0 to use kv.rangefeed.closed_timestamp_refresh_intervalcapped at kv.rangefeed.closed_timestamp_refresh_interval
kv.rangefeed.concurrent_catchup_iterators	16	16	number of rangefeeds catchup iterators a store will allow concurrently before queueing
kv.rangefeed.enabled	false	false	if set, rangefeed registration is enabled
kv.rangefeed.max_scan_parallelism	64	64	maximum number of concurrent scan requests that can be issued during initial scan
kv.rangefeed.memory_budgets.enabled	true	true	if set, rangefeed memory budgets are enabled
kv.rangefeed.push_txns.barrier.enabled	true	true	flush and apply prior writes when a txn push returns an ambiguous abort (disabling may emit premature checkpoints before writes in rare cases)
kv.rangefeed.push_txns.enabled	true	true	periodically push txn write timestamps to advance rangefeed resolved timestamps
kv.rangefeed.range_stuck_threshold	1m0s	1m0s	restart rangefeeds if they don't emit anything for the specified threshold; 0 disables (kv.rangefeed.closed_timestamp_refresh_interval takes precedence)
kv.rangefeed.scheduler.enabled	true	true	use shared fixed pool of workers for all range feeds instead of a worker per range (worker pool size is determined by COCKROACH_RANGEFEED_SCHEDULER_WORKERS env variable)
kv.replica_circuit_breaker.slow_replication_threshold	1m0s	1m0s	duration after which slow proposals trip the per-Replica circuit breaker (zero duration disables breakers)
kv.replica_gc_queue.enabled	true	true	whether the replica gc queue is enabled
kv.replica_stats.addsst_request_size_factor	50000	50000	the divisor that is applied to addsstable request sizes, then recorded in a leaseholders QPS; 0 means all requests are treated as cost 1
kv.replicate_queue.enabled	true	true	whether the replicate queue is enabled
kv.replication_reports.interval	1m0s	1m0s	the frequency for generating the replication_constraint_stats, replication_stats_report and replication_critical_localities reports (set to 0 to disable)
kv.snapshot.external_files.enabled	false	false	enables sending external files as metadata during a snapshot
kv.snapshot.ingest_as_write_threshold	100 KiB	100 KiB	size below which a range snapshot ingestion will be performed as a normal write
kv.snapshot_delegation.max_delegation_attempts	1	1	the max number of delegates to attempt for sending snapshots, before falling back to sending from the leaseholder. set to 0 to disable delegated snapshots.
kv.snapshot_delegations.queue_limit	3	3	the max requests a delegate can queue before rejecting an additional delegation request. set to -1 to allow unlimited requests
kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate	1.0 MiB	32 MiB	the rate limit (bytes/sec) to use for rebalance and upreplication snapshots
kv.snapshot_receiver.excise.enabled	true	true	set to false to disable excises in place of range deletions for KV snapshots
kv.snapshot_receiver.reservation_queue_timeout_fraction	0.4	0.4	the fraction of a snapshot's total timeout that it is allowed to spend queued on the receiver waiting for a reservation
kv.snapshot_sender.batch_size	256 KiB	256 KiB	size of key-value batches sent over the network during snapshots
kv.snapshot_sst.sync_size	512 KiB	512 KiB	threshold after which snapshot SST writes must fsync
kv.split.estimated_mvcc_stats.enabled	true	true	if enabled, MVCC stats will be computed estimated (as opposed to computed accurately) during splits
kv.split.max_mvcc_stat_bytes_diff	5120000	5120000	defines the max number of bytes that are acceptable for an individual MVCC stat to diverge; needs kv.split.estimated_mvcc_stats.enabled to be true
kv.split.max_mvcc_stat_count_diff	5000	5000	defines the max number of units that are acceptable for an individual MVCC stat to diverge; needs kv.split.estimated_mvcc_stats.enabled to be true
kv.split.mvcc_stats_recomputation.enabled	true	true	if enabled, MVCC stats will be recomputed at the beginning of a split to prevent stats estimates from drifting
kv.split.slow_split_tracing_threshold	2s	2s	the duration after which a trace of the split is logged
kv.split_queue.enabled	true	true	whether the split queue is enabled
kv.store.system_config_update.queue_add_burst	32	32	the burst rate at which the store will add all replicas to the split and merge queue due to system config gossip
kv.store.system_config_update.queue_add_rate	0.5	0.5	the rate (per second) at which the store will add, all replicas to the split and merge queue due to system config gossip
kv.streamer.concurrency_limit	320	320	maximum number of asynchronous requests by a single streamer
kv.tenant_rate_limiter.burst_limit_seconds	10	10	per-tenant burst limit as a multiplier of the rate
kv.tenant_rate_limiter.rate_limit	-400	-400	per-tenant rate limit in KV Compute Units per second if positive, or KV Compute Units per second per CPU if negative
kv.tenant_rate_limiter.read_batch_cost	0.567	0.567	base cost of a read batch in KV Compute Units
kv.tenant_rate_limiter.read_cost_per_mebibyte	12.77	12.77	cost of a read in KV Compute Units per MiB
kv.tenant_rate_limiter.read_request_cost	0.0275	0.0275	base cost of a read request in KV Compute Units
kv.tenant_rate_limiter.write_batch_cost	0.328	0.328	base cost of a write batch in KV Compute Units
kv.tenant_rate_limiter.write_cost_per_megabyte	43.95	43.95	cost of a write in KV Compute Units per MiB
kv.tenant_rate_limiter.write_request_cost	0.314	0.314	base cost of a write request in KV Compute Units
kv.timeseries_maintenance_queue.enabled	true	true	whether the timeseries maintenance queue is enabled
kv.trace.slow_request_stacks.threshold	30s	30s	duration spent in processing above any available stack history is appended to its trace, if automatic trace snapshots are enabled
kv.trace.snapshot.enable_threshold	0s	0s	enables tracing and gathers timing information on all snapshots;snapshots with a duration longer than this threshold will have their trace logged (set to 0 to disable);
kv.transaction.dropping_latches_before_eval.enabled	true	true	if enabled, allows certain read-only KV requests to drop latches before they evaluate
kv.transaction.internal.max_auto_retries	100	100	controls the maximum number of auto-retries an internal KV transaction will perform before aborting and returning an error. Can be set to 0 to disable any retry attempt.
kv.transaction.keep_refresh_spans_on_savepoint_rollback.enabled	false	false	if enabled, all refresh spans accumulated since a savepoint was created are kept after the savepoint is rolled back
kv.transaction.max_intents_bytes	4194304	4194304	maximum number of bytes used to track locks in transactions
kv.transaction.max_refresh_spans_bytes	4194304	4194304	maximum number of bytes used to track refresh spans in serializable transactions
kv.transaction.parallel_commits.enabled	true	true	if enabled, transactional commits will be parallelized with transactional writes
kv.transaction.reject_over_max_intents_budget.enabled	false	false	if set, transactions that exceed their lock tracking budget (kv.transaction.max_intents_bytes) are rejected instead of having their lock spans imprecisely compressed
kv.transaction.write_pipelining.enabled	true	true	if enabled, transactional writes are pipelined through Raft consensus
kv.transaction.write_pipelining.locking_reads.enabled	true	true	if enabled, transactional locking reads are pipelined through Raft consensus
kv.transaction.write_pipelining.max_batch_size	128	128	if non-zero, defines that maximum size batch that will be pipelined through Raft consensus
kv.transaction.write_pipelining.ranged_writes.enabled	true	true	if enabled, transactional ranged writes are pipelined through Raft consensus
kv.transfer_expiration_leases_first.enabled	true	true	controls whether we tr
